dimensions.label: Process Density
dimensions.description: Accumulated weight of steps, reviews, approvals, handoffs, and procedural requirements relative to what the underlying work requires.

dimensions.label: Systems Friction
dimensions.description: Labor and attention consumed by system switching, duplicate data entry, manual patching, and integration gaps.

dimensions.label: Reporting and Exception Burden
dimensions.description: Operating labor absorbed by reporting, oversight, proof-of-work demands, and exception handling.

dimensions.label: Workaround Dependence
dimensions.description: Degree to which execution depends on off-path effort, informal coordination, or improvised compensatory behavior.

dimensions.label: Control Load
dimensions.description: Cumulative governance weight relative to the actual risk profile and value of the work being controlled.

dimensions.label: Administrative Upkeep Burden
dimensions.description: Ongoing maintenance labor required simply to keep the administrative environment functioning.

notes.runtime_state_trigger: State keys resolve against the running engine state object and are evaluated after burden recomputation and before next-item selection.
notes.maxFirings: Scoped to the injected probe or item ID, not merely the routing rule.
notes.dedup: The engine deduplicates the queue by item ID before each render step.
notes.skipIfAnswered: If true, do not inject an item already answered earlier in the same run.

60.coverageRule: All six dimensions, deepest on top 3 active dimensions

text.operational: When you think about where work slows down or requires extra effort, what is the most common source?
text.managerial: When you think about where your team loses time or absorbs unnecessary administrative effort, what is the most common source?
text.senior_leader: When you think about where the operating environment is imposing the most burden on execution, what is the most common source?

items.scoringNote: Primary burden source is the initial gating classifier and should activate the selected dimension immediately.

text.operational: Roughly how much of your working time goes toward administrative tasks rather than directly advancing the work itself?
text.managerial: Roughly how much of your team’s time goes toward administrative tasks rather than directly advancing the work itself?
text.senior_leader: Roughly how much capacity in this environment is being absorbed by administrative tasks rather than directly advancing the work itself?

items.scoringNote: High admin time share activates upkeep burden and may also indicate hidden reporting burden.

text.operational: Over the past year, has the administrative weight of your environment gotten heavier, lighter, or stayed about the same?
text.managerial: Over the past year, has the administrative burden around this workflow gotten heavier, lighter, or stayed about the same?
text.senior_leader: Over the past year, has the administrative burden around this operating environment gotten heavier, lighter, or stayed about the same?

items.scoringNote: Trajectory is a burden-age and growth signal rather than a direct burden dimension.

text.operational: When administrative burden absorbs time in this environment, is that cost generally visible to people who could reduce it?
text.managerial: When your team loses capacity to administrative burden, is that cost generally visible to people who could reduce it?
text.senior_leader: When administrative burden absorbs capacity in this environment, is that cost generally visible to the people who have authority to reduce it?

items.scoringNote: Hidden drag visibility suppresses confidence and increases the chance of hidden burden profile classification.

text.operational: How many distinct approval, review, or sign-off steps does a routine work item typically require before it is complete?
text.managerial: How many distinct approval, review, or sign-off steps does a routine work item typically require in this environment?
text.senior_leader: How many distinct approval, review, or sign-off steps does a routine item typically require in this operating environment?

items.scoringNote: High step count is a direct process-density indicator.

text.managerial: How often do exceptions or escalations become part of the normal path rather than the routine path?
text.senior_leader: How common is exception handling relative to the routine path in this environment?

items.scoringNote: High exception ratio cross-loads into reporting and exception burden.

text.operational: How many separate systems or tools does a person typically touch to complete one unit of work in this environment?
text.managerial: How many separate systems or tools does your team typically touch to complete one unit of work in this environment?
text.senior_leader: How many distinct systems or tools are typically involved in moving one unit of work through this environment?

items.scoringNote: System count increases friction exposure but should be interpreted together with design quality.

text.operational: How often does completing work require re-entering information that already exists somewhere else in the system?
text.managerial: How often does your team have to re-enter information that already exists elsewhere in the system environment?
text.senior_leader: How often does this environment require duplicate data entry across systems?

items.scoringNote: Duplicate entry is a direct signal of systems friction and may activate contradiction probe C3 in combination with low system count.

text.operational: How many hours per week does a typical person in this environment spend on status reporting, recurring updates, or administrative documentation?
text.managerial: How many hours per week does a typical team member spend on status reporting, recurring updates, or administrative documentation?
text.senior_leader: How many hours per week does a typical person in this environment spend on reporting, recurring updates, or administrative documentation?

items.scoringNote: High reporting hours are a primary direct measure of reporting burden.

text.managerial: Has exception handling become normalized enough that it now behaves like part of the standard operating load?
text.senior_leader: Has exception processing become normalized enough that it now behaves like part of the baseline operating design?

items.scoringNote: High exception normalization combined with flat trajectory activates chronic normalization contradiction logic.

text.operational: How often do people have to manually follow up just to keep work moving?
text.managerial: How often does your team have to manually follow up just to keep work moving?
text.senior_leader: How often does this environment depend on manual follow-up simply to preserve movement?

items.scoringNote: Manual follow-up is one half of contradiction probe C1 and should remain distinct from off-path work.

text.operational: How often does getting work done require steps or contacts that are not part of the official process?
text.managerial: How often does your team rely on off-path effort or informal coordination to get work done?
text.senior_leader: How often does this environment depend on off-path effort or informal coordination to preserve output?

items.scoringNote: High off-path work is a primary workaround dependence signal and pairs with manual follow-up in contradiction probe C1.

text.operational: For the work in this environment, does the governance and control overhead feel proportionate to the actual risk or compliance need?
text.managerial: Does the governance and control overhead around this work feel proportionate to the actual risk or compliance need?
text.senior_leader: Does the current governance and control overhead appear proportionate to the actual risk and value profile of the work?

options.label: Much heavier than the risk warrants

items.scoringNote: Governance fit is the primary control-load proportionality test and one half of contradiction probe C2.

text.managerial: When new controls or oversight requirements are added, how often are existing ones removed to compensate?
text.senior_leader: When new controls or oversight requirements are added, how often are existing ones removed to compensate?

items.scoringNote: Control add/remove asymmetry is the bias half of contradiction probe C2.

text.senior_leader: When uncertainty appears, what is the institution’s default first response?

items.scoringNote: Senior leader directional control-bias probe that can also surface C2 directly.

text.operational: How much of the weekly administrative workload here is upkeep rather than work that advances outcomes?
text.managerial: How much of the weekly administrative workload in this environment is upkeep rather than work that advances outcomes?
text.senior_leader: How much of the weekly administrative workload in this environment is upkeep rather than work that advances outcomes?

options.label: Upkeep takes more time than expected

items.scoringNote: Upkeep share is the first direct burden framing question for D6.

text.operational: When work requires off-path effort, does that usually involve follow-up steps that would not show in the formal process?
text.managerial: Does your team’s off-path effort often happen outside what the formal workflow would record as follow-up?
text.senior_leader: Is it possible the informal effort sustaining this workflow is not visible in formal workflow records?

options.label: No, the formal and informal routes are reasonably aligned

items.scoringNote: Cross-item contradiction probe for visible/invisible labor split.

text.operational: Even when the process seems broadly reasonable, is the normal response to uncertainty still to add another check, step, or approval?
text.managerial: Even where governance appears generally proportionate, does your environment still default to adding new oversight more easily than removing old oversight?
text.senior_leader: Even where leaders describe governance as right-sized, is the institution’s default response to uncertainty still to add control rather than redesign the workflow?

items.scoringNote: Cross-item contradiction probe for framing-versus-behavior gap in control load.

text.operational: If the number of systems is not especially high, is the real problem how those systems are designed, connected, or used in practice?
text.managerial: Does the friction seem to come less from system count and more from weak integration, poor workflow design, or manual bridging between systems?
text.senior_leader: Is the technology burden in this environment being driven more by design quality and integration gaps than by the sheer number of systems involved?

items.scoringNote: Cross-item contradiction probe for design-quality versus system-count mismatch.

text.operational: Could the real burden here be heavier than it appears because some of the extra effort is simply treated as normal work?
text.managerial: Is it possible your team is absorbing more administrative burden than leadership can clearly see because it is hidden inside normal delivery expectations?
text.senior_leader: Could this environment be materially heavier in practice than current signals suggest because the upkeep cost is not visible in capacity accounting?

items.scoringNote: Scoring-layer contradiction probe. Evaluate only after burden recomputation and before next-item selection.

text.operational: Has this level of exception handling been part of the job long enough that it no longer stands out as unusual?
text.managerial: Has recurring exception handling become normalized enough that the team now treats it as standard operating load rather than a signal of workflow weakness?
text.senior_leader: Has exception processing become so routine that the organization now experiences it as baseline operating design rather than a condition worth correcting?

items.scoringNote: Contradiction probe for flat trajectory plus normalized exception burden.

rules: Top 2 burden dimensions are stable and clearly separated from the rest.
Contradictions have been resolved or there are no unresolved contradiction flags.
Minimum depth requirements have been satisfied.
One additional item is unlikely to materially change the primary burden diagnosis or first intervention order.

stabilityDefinition.minimumSignals: At least two burden-bearing signals, or one high-weight numeric signal plus one confirming categorical signal.
stabilityDefinition.confidenceThreshold: Must exceed the threshold for the selected depth.
stabilityDefinition.rankStability: Rank position unchanged across the last two scoring updates unless a contradiction probe is still open.

burdenProfileShapes.concentrated: One burden dimension dominates. Emphasize focus and first-move leverage.
burdenProfileShapes.distributed: Multiple dimensions elevated together. Emphasize sequencing discipline and risk of burden displacement.
burdenProfileShapes.hidden: Formal signals may understate the real condition. Emphasize invisible labor, visibility gap, and calibrated uncertainty.

implementationPath.note: Feed the existing scoreOperationalSystems.js through a legacy-key adapter first, then rewrite the scorer to consume the new bank after parity is confirmed.
